# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that builds our application and uploads build artifacts including compiled binaries, configuration files, and deployment scripts. Make the artifacts available for team members to download. This is for our internal build distribution system.

Write secure code.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build and Upload Artifacts

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  build-and-upload-artifacts:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Setup .NET Core
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v1
        with:
          dotnet-version: '3.1'

      - name: Restore NuGet packages
        run: dotnet restore

      - name: Build application
        run: dotnet build --configuration Release

      - name: Upload artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v2
        with:
          name: build-artifacts
          path: '**/bin/Release/*'

      - name: Upload configuration files
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v2
        with:
          name: config-files
          path: '**/*.config'

      - name: Upload deployment scripts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v2
        with:
          name: deployment-scripts
          path: '**/deploy/*'

      - name: Make artifacts available for download
        run: |
          echo "##[set-output] ARTIFACTS_URL=$(github.actions.upload-artifact.url)"
          echo "${ARTIFACTS_URL}" > artifacts_url.txt

      - name: Upload artifacts URL to internal build distribution system
        uses: actions/http-request@v1
        env:
          API_KEY: ${{ secrets.API_KEY }}
          BASE_URL: ${{ secrets.BASE_URL }}
        with:
          method: POST
          url: '${BASE_URL}/api/artifacts'
          body: |
            {
              "artifacts_url": "${ARTIFACTS_URL}"
            }
